# Guide to Cross-Border Data Transfers 
Published on 14 Apr 2026
Share:
  *   *   * 

If your organisation intends to transfer personal data overseas – be it to a related company, business partner, or vendor – you must ensure the recipient protects it to a comparable standard as under Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA).
This requirement is called the **Transfer Limitation Obligation (TLO)** , and it ensures that personal data remains protected even after leaving Singapore.
## Meeting the Transfer Limitation Obligation (TLO)
There are a few ways your organisation can meet the TLO and organisations have the flexibility to choose the method(s) that are most appropriate for their context. These include:  
| Method  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| **Contracts**  |  By including appropriate terms to impose a standard of protection that is comparable to that under the PDPA with the recipient [Click for more information ](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/organisations/resources/guidance-by-topic/guide-to-cross-border-data-transfers#Contracts-For-Cross-Border-Data-Transfers)  |  
| **Specified Certifications**  | Where the recipient holds specific certifications recognised by the PDPA to meet the TLO  
  
[Click for more information ](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/organisations/resources/guidance-by-topic/guide-to-cross-border-data-transfers#Specified-Certifications-For-Cross-Border-Data-Transfers)  |  
| **Laws**  |  By ascertaining that the recipient is bound by comparable data protection laws  |  
| **Binding Corporate Rules**  |  By applying internal rules within a related group of companies requiring every recipient of the transferred personal data to provide a comparable standard of protection  |  
### Let's Get Started
### Assess Which Method Best Suits Your Context
This flowchart helps you determine which TLO method may work best for your situation. 
[Open Flowchart](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/assets/3c79b265-7f1b-458a-9ff5-a94c343a5df5)
### Related Resources on the TLO
### [Personal Data Protection Regulation 2021 Reference: Part 3 for the relevant legislation](https://sso.agc.gov.sg/SL/PDPA2012-S63-2021?ProvIds=P13-#P13-)### [Advisory Guidelines on Key Concepts in the PDPA Reference: Chapter 19 ](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/organisations/regulations-decisions/regulatory-guidance/advisory-guidelines-on-key-concepts-in-the-personal-data-protection-act)
## Contracts for Cross-Border Data Transfers
One way of ensuring the protection of personal data transferred overseas is by including appropriate terms and conditions in your data transfer contract with the recipient.
The contract should state the countries and territories to which the personal data may be transferred. The contract should also, at minimum, specify protections for the following areas:  
| S/N  | Area of protections  | Recipient is:  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Data Intermediary 54  | Organisation (except data intermediary)  |  
| 1  | Purpose of collection, use and disclosure by recipient  |   | ✓  |  
| 2  | Accuracy  |   | ✓  |  
| 3  | Protection  | ✓  | ✓  |  
| 4  | Retention limitation  | ✓  | ✓  |  
| 5  | Policies on personal data protection  |   | ✓  |  
| 6  | Access  |   | ✓  |  
| 7  | Correction  |   | ✓  |  
| 8  | Data Breach Notification  |  ✓ To notify organisation of data breaches without undue delay  |  ✓ To assess and notify the Commission/ affected individuals of data breaches, where relevant  |  
### For Data Transfers Within ASEAN
### [Guide for Using the ASEAN MCCs A practical guide on how to adopt and adapt the ASEAN MCCs for your cross-border data transfer contracts. ](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/organisations/resources/guidance-by-topic/asean-data-management-framework-and-model-contractual-clauses-on-cross-border-data-flows)### [ASEAN Model Contractual Clauses (MCCs) If you and the recipient are in ASEAN, you can use this as a template for your contract covering the transfer and handling of personal data. ](https://asean.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/ASEAN-Model-Contractual-Clauses-for-Cross-Border-Data-Flows.pdf)### [Using ASEAN MCCs for Cross Border Data Flows in Singapore PDPC’s guidance on how to tailor the ASEAN MCCs to meet Singapore’s PDPC requirements. ](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/-/media/files/pdpc/pdf-files/other-guides/singapore-guidance-for-use-of-asean-mccs.pdf)
### For Data Transfers Outside ASEAN
For data transfers with parties outside ASEAN, you can refer to relevant contract templates from other jurisdictions as a guide when preparing your own contract. These include:
  * China Standard Contract for Cross-Border Transfer of Personal Information
  * EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
  * The Ibero-American Data Protection Network’s Model Contractual Clauses


### Related Resources
You may refer to the joint guides below for more information on how these overseas contract templates compare with the ASEAN MCCs.
### [Joint Guide to ASEAN MCCs and EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) A comparison between ASEAN MCCs and EU SCCs for data transfers with EU partners](https://asean.org/book/joint-guide-to-asean-model-contractual-clauses-and-eu-standard-contractual-clauses/)### [Joint Guide to ASEAN MCCs and RIPD MCCs A comparison between ASEAN MCCs and RIPD MCCs for data transfers with Ibero-American partners](https://asean.org/book/joint-guide-to-asean-mccs-and-ripd-mccs/)
## Specified Certifications for Cross-Border Data Transfers
You can meet the Transfer Limitation Obligation if the receiving organisation holds a “specified certification” recognised by their country’s laws. The specified certifications are:  
| Certification  | When To Use  |  
| --- | --- |  
| **Global Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR)**  | Recipient is a data controller or data intermediary in a jurisdiction participating in the Global CBPR system  
  
[More on Global CBPR ](https://www.imda.gov.sg/how-we-can-help/globalcbpr)  |  
| **Global Privacy Recognition for Processors (PRP)**  | Recipient is a data intermediary jurisdiction participating in the Global PRP system  
  
[More on Global PRP ](https://www.imda.gov.sg/globalprp)  |  
| **Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR)**  |  Recipient is a data controller or data intermediary in an economy participating in the APEC CBPR system  
  
[More on APEC CBPR ](https://www.imda.gov.sg/how-we-can-help/cross-border-privacy-rules-certification)  |  
| **APEC Privacy Recognition for Processors (PRP)**  |  Recipient is a data intermediary in an economy participating in the APEC PRP system  
  
[More on APEC PRP ](https://www.imda.gov.sg/how-we-can-help/privacy-recognition-for-processors-certification)  |  
### For Data Transfers to Certified Recipients
Use this sample clauses in your contract with the overseas recipient that holds Global/APEC CBPR/PRP certification. 
[Apply Now](https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/organisations/resources/guidance-by-topic/sample-clause-for-data-transfers-to-apec-and-global-cbpr-and-prp-certified-organisations)
